title: quercus × tlemcenensis f. maroccana
intro: Quercus × tlemcenensis f. maroccana, also known as the Tlemcen oak, is a rare and unique deciduous tree hybrid belonging to the Quercus genus, native to the Atlas Mountains in Morocco. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its bark and leaves, which are rich in flavonoids and tannins that have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Quercus × tlemcenensis f. maroccana include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in the production of various herbal preparations, including capsules, tablets, and topical creams.
